title: Decision lifecycle has no broadened-by state — superseded enum mis-fits enacted-then-broadened transitions
description: The decisions schema declares status enum [open, enacted, superseded] with the description characterizing superseded as withdrawn-without-enactment / not-a-real-decision. This covers open -> superseded cleanly but does not cover enacted -> superseded cleanly when the predecessor was a correct, ratified decision whose scope is later broadened by a successor decision (the case for DEC-0012 superseded by DEC-0013 on 2026-05-07). The schema forces this transition into the same superseded state with no way to distinguish retracted from broadened-by.

evidence.reference: DEC-0006/0007/0010 (open->superseded, fits) vs DEC-0012 (enacted->superseded, semantic stretch)

impact: Substrate-level integrity ambiguity: a downstream reading status=superseded cannot distinguish retired-as-never-real from correctly-enacted-then-broadened. Lineage recoverable via supersedes/superseded_by fields but lifecycle vocabulary loses signal. Recurs as pi-context config-driven vocabulary matures (block_kinds, relation_types, status_buckets all evolve via broadening).
proposed_resolution: Add a fourth status enum value (extended or broadened) for enacted -> broadened-by-successor. Update description distinguishing superseded (retired without ratification) from extended (correctly enacted, scope broadened by successor, original preserved). Transition rules: superseded reachable from open; extended reachable from enacted. Couples to FGAP-006 (schema versioning).
